text:
The core type inference engine in the Rust compiler is spread across three key files:

1. **`compiler/rustc_infer/src/infer/mod.rs`** — Defines `InferCtxt<'tcx>` (line 231), the main inference context struct. It also defines `InferCtxtInner<'tcx>` (line 89) which holds internal mutable state (undo logs, projection cache, type/region/const variable tables), and `InferCtxtBuilder<'tcx>` (line 508) which constructs an `InferCtxt`.

2. **`compiler/rustc_hir_typeck/src/fn_ctxt/mod.rs`** — Defines `FnCtxt<'a, 'tcx>` (line 44), the function-level type checking context. It is declared `pub(crate)` and wraps an `InferCtxt` to provide function-body-scoped type checking, including expected return types, divergence tracking, and coroutine info.

3. **`compiler/rustc_infer/src/infer/type_variable.rs`** — Defines `TypeVariableOrigin` (line 94), a struct containing a `Span` and an optional `DefId` for the type parameter. This records how/why a type inference variable was created. The file also defines `TypeVariableData` (line 103) which stores the origin alongside the variable's state.

The dependency chain flows: `FnCtxt` wraps `InferCtxt`, which internally manages type variables via `TypeVariableOrigin`.
